evals.prompt: We're on Drupal 10.3.8 and need to upgrade to Drupal 11. Our site has 15 custom modules, uses Classy as a base theme, and we haven't run any deprecation scans yet. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: Should walk through Phase 1 (Assess): update to latest 10.3.x patch, install upgrade_status, run PHPStan, check contrib readiness. Should flag Classy as a known blocker (removed in D11) and recommend Starterkit migration. Should NOT enumerate specific removed D11 modules from memory — should say upgrade_status will identify those.
assertions: Recommends installing and running upgrade_status module as the first step
Recommends running PHPStan or drupal-check on custom code
Flags Classy base theme as needing migration
Suggests Starterkit theme generation as the replacement approach
Does NOT produce a hardcoded list of removed D11 modules — defers to upgrade_status
References the upgrade rule files from the rules library

evals.prompt: Drupal Rector fixed most of our deprecations but we still have some hook_form_alter and hook_node_presave functions in .module files. Are those going to break in the next major version?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that procedural hooks still work but are being deprecated in favor of OOP #[Hook] classes. Should reference 14-oop-hooks.md. Should recommend migrating to OOP hooks proactively but clarify they won't break immediately in the next major — the OOP hook system is the direction, not yet a hard requirement for all hooks.
assertions: Explains the OOP hook migration direction without claiming procedural hooks are immediately broken
Shows the #[Hook] attribute pattern as the replacement
Recommends proactive migration as part of upgrade prep

evals.prompt: Our composer update to the next major version failed with dependency conflicts. How do we debug this?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `composer why-not drupal/core:^NEXT` to identify what is blocking. Should explain that contrib modules pinning old core versions are the most common cause. Should suggest checking each blocking module for a compatible release, applying a patch, or finding an alternative.
assertions: Recommends composer why-not as the diagnostic tool
Identifies contrib version constraints as the most common blocker
Suggests checking drupal.org for compatible releases
Mentions patching or finding alternatives as fallback options
Does not suggest force-overriding Composer constraints
